title: Ctenitis dimidiata
taxonomy: Ctenitis dimidiata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is classified within the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, consisting of over 2,000 species. Ctenitis dimidiata is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its distinctive fronds and growth habits. Classification is a critical component of understanding the evolution and relationships between different plant species.
uses: Ctenitis dimidiata is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. The plant is believed to help in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it is used to treat wounds and skin infections due to its antimicrobial properties. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Ctenitis dimidiata in modern medicine.

preparations: Ctenitis dimidiata is a plant species that has been used in traditional herbal medicine.
